changed.from: For all large n the two laws P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} of def:two-point-witness lie in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma): they satisfy ass:iid, ass:consistency, ass:unconfoundedness, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin (margin tail with constant C_m via mass <= C_B h_n^alpha on B_n only), ass:zero-effect, and ass:overlap-decay (via the tight-window envelope equality of prop:overlap-envelope at beta=beta_ag), with pi_star in Pi for both.
changed.to: For all large n the corrected witness laws P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} satisfy ass:iid, ass:consistency, ass:unconfoundedness, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin, ass:zero-effect, and ass:overlap-decay. If, in addition, the two explicit witness-optimal policies x -> 1 and x -> 1{x notin B_n} belong to Pi, then P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} lie in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma).
changed.reason: As stated, class membership is false for arbitrary finite-VC Pi. The corrected two-point witness has pi_star,+ = 1 and pi_star,- = 1{x notin B_n}. A finite-VC class can exclude one or both of these policies; for example Pi={1} is finite VC, and then P_{n,-} cannot belong to P_class because its optimal policy is not in Pi. The other member-properties are derivable from the corrected witness, but the optimal-in-class clause needs an explicit policy-richness restriction.

changed.from: M_n(alpha,gamma) = inf_{pi_hat} sup_{P in P_class} E_P R_P(pi_hat) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n, with c > 0 independent of n.
changed.to: Assume the two explicit witness-optimal policies of the corrected def:two-point-witness, namely x -> 1 and x -> 1{x notin B_n}, belong to Pi for all large n. Then M_n(alpha,gamma) = inf_{pi_hat} sup_{P in P_class} E_P R_P(pi_hat) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n, with c > 0 independent of n.
changed.reason: The current theorem is false for arbitrary finite-VC Pi. If Pi={1}, then every P in P_class must satisfy pi_star=1 almost surely, so the unique Pi-valued estimator has zero regret and M_n(alpha,gamma)=0, contradicting any positive lower bound. The only missing ingredient is witness policy richness: once the two witness-optimal policies belong to Pi, the corrected two-point construction yields the Le Cam lower bound.

changed.from: Under ass:overlap-decay, ass:policy-class (finite VC), A5 and ass:vc-localized-envelope: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= U_n(alpha,gamma; etahat) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}, with c,C>0 independent of n. Moreover r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu) = r_star(alpha,gamma) iff kappa_mu = 1/2; at kappa_mu=1/2 the matched exponent n^{-r_star} holds, while for kappa_mu<1/2 the bounds exhibit a strict oracle/feasible separation.
changed.to: Under ass:overlap-decay, ass:policy-class, ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope, and ass:vc-localized-envelope, and assuming the two explicit witness-optimal policies of the corrected def:two-point-witness belong to Pi for all large n, we have c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= U_n(alpha,gamma; etahat) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}, with c,C>0 independent of n. Moreover r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu) = r_star(alpha,gamma) iff kappa_mu = 1/2; at kappa_mu=1/2 the matched exponent n^{-r_star} holds, while for kappa_mu<1/2 the bounds exhibit a strict oracle/feasible separation.
changed.reason: The current corollary omits two hypotheses needed for the upper half, namely ass:known-logging-partition and ass:cell-constant-nuisance, because thm:feasible-upper depends on both. Its lower half also inherits the policy-richness defect of thm:minimax-lower: without the two witness-optimal policies in Pi, the claimed positive minimax lower bound can fail outright. The narrowed statement adds exactly the missing hypotheses used by the proved upper and lower ingredients.

changed.from: s_feas = min{1/(2+alpha+beta_ag), kappa_mu/(1+kappa_mu(alpha+beta_ag))}; r_feas = (1+alpha) s_feas; hbar_n = n^{-s_feas}; rhobar_n = hbar_n^{1+alpha}; qbar_n = (q_0 if gamma=0 else hbar_n^{beta_ag}).
changed.to: s_feas = min{1/(2+alpha+beta_ag), kappa_mu/(1+kappa_mu(alpha+beta_ag))}; r_feas = (1+alpha) s_feas; hbar_n = n^{-s_feas}; rhobar_n = hbar_n^{1+alpha}; qbar_n = (1/4 if beta_ag = 0 else hbar_n^{beta_ag}).
changed.reason: The current formula references an undefined symbol q_0 and is invalid when alpha=0 and gamma>0: then beta_ag=0, so the displayed gamma>0 branch gives qbar_n=hbar_n^0=1, which is outside the admissible clipping range (0,1/2]. The proof only needs a fixed positive clip floor whenever beta_ag=0, so replacing the stale q_0 branch by an explicit constant such as 1/4 corrects both defects.

changed.from: Vhat_{n,qbar_n}(pi) = n^{-1} sum_i pi(X_i) Gammahat_{qbar_n}(O_i) with cross-fitted score; pi_hat_n in argmax_{pi in Pi} Vhat_{n,qbar_n}(pi).
changed.to: Vhat_{n,qbar_n}(pi) = n^{-1} sum_i pi(X_i) Gammahat_{qbar_n}(O_i), where Gammahat_{qbar_n} is the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW score built as follows: on each training fold and each cell C in the known partition C_n, set the propensity estimate equal to the known cellwise logging propensity e_P(C), and set the outcome-regression estimates hatmu_a^{(-k)} on C to the truncated training-fold empirical means among observations with A=a and X in C. Then pi_hat_n is any argmax of Vhat_{n,qbar_n}(pi) over pi in Pi.
changed.reason: The current definition leaves the fitted score Gammahat unspecified. The upper proof uses the known-logging/cellwise-mean construction in an essential way: it needs exact propensity knowledge to make the clipped-score bias vanish off the rare-arm region, and it needs cellwise bounded-mean error to invoke the rare-arm envelope. Making the estimator map explicit is a correction to the construction, not a scope change.

changed.from: On X=[0,1] with P_X=Lebesgue, fix h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)}, q_n=(q_0 if gamma=0 else h_n^{beta_ag}), and an active block B_n=[0, c_B h_n^alpha] with P_X(B_n)=c_B h_n^alpha. The two laws P_{n,sigma}, sigma in {+,-}, share P_X, share the logging propensity e(x)=q_n on B_n and e(x)=1/2 off B_n, and share the off-block outcome law (contrast tau_0 in (0,1)). On the treated active cell {X in B_n, A=1} the outcome Y in {-1,1} is Bernoulli with P(Y=1)= (1 + sigma h_n)/2, giving tau_{P_{n,sigma}}=sigma h_n on B_n and tau=tau_0 off B_n; the laws agree everywhere off the treated active cell. Constants are chosen with 8 C_B C_Q < log 5.
changed.to: On X=[0,1] with P_X=Lebesgue, fix h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)}, q_n=(1/4 if beta_ag=0 else h_n^{beta_ag}), and an active block B_n=[0, c_B h_n^alpha] with P_X(B_n)=c_B h_n^alpha. The two laws P_{n,sigma}, sigma in {+,-}, share P_X, share the logging propensity e(x)=q_n on B_n and e(x)=1/2 off B_n, and share an off-block outcome law with common optimal label 1 and contrast tau_0 > u_0. On B_n set mu_0(x)=0 and let the treated outcome on the active treated cell {X in B_n, A=1} satisfy P(Y=1 | X in B_n, A=1)=(1+sigma h_n)/2, so tau_{P_{n,sigma}}=sigma h_n on B_n. The laws agree everywhere off the treated active cell. Constants are chosen with 8 C_B C_Q < log 5.
changed.reason: The current witness has two independent defects. First, it again uses the stale q_0 branch and produces q_n=1 when beta_ag=0 and gamma>0. Second, the proof of margin and overlap membership needs the off-block contrast to lie above the margin window so that only B_n contributes to the small-effect events, but the present text merely says tau_0 in (0,1) and does not relate tau_0 to u_0. The corrected construction fixes the clip floor and makes the off-block regime explicit enough for the membership proof.

note: Orchestrator approved all 6 D0-SOLVE proposals. 3 narrowings add the STANDARD policy-richness condition (Pi contains the witness-optimal policies x->1 and x->1{x notin B_n}) — the honest, non-laundered replacement for the dropped ass:primitive-cube label-flexibility clause (false otherwise: Pi={1} => M_n=0). 3 def corrections: q_0 clip floor -> 1/4 when beta_ag=0 (alpha=0,gamma>0 edge); tau_0>u_0 off-block; explicit cross-fit cellwise estimator. All direction-of-truth (narrow/correct); none re-introduce the construction-existence laundering.

changed.from: Let P_KL be the known-logging design subclass: laws P in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma) that additionally satisfy ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope (design granularity), and ass:boundary-smoothness. Under ass:vc-localized-envelope, the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n of def:feasible-erm satisfies sup_{P in P_KL} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}, with C>0 independent of n, where r_feas is DERIVED (not assumed) from the boundary smoothness kappa_mu and the design granularity.
changed.to: Let P_KL^loc be the subclass of laws P in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma) that satisfy ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope, ass:boundary-smoothness, and the boundary-local clipping condition P_X({x : p_P(x) <= 2 qbar_n, |tau_P(x)| > hbar_n}) = 0. Under ass:vc-localized-envelope, the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n of def:feasible-erm satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}, with C>0 independent of n.
changed.reason: As stated, the theorem is false on the frozen class. The current primitives allow a law with a positive-mass rare-arm cell C of diameter 0, propensity e_P(C)=1/(n P_X(C)) <= 2 qbar_n, and contrast tau_P(C)=tau_0>u_0, while all small-effect events are empty so ass:margin and ass:overlap-decay hold trivially. This law also satisfies ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope (since n P_X(C) e_P(C)=1), and ass:boundary-smoothness (vacuous on the atomic rare region). For the policy class Pi={1, 1_{X notin C}}, the optimal policy is 1, but the feasible estimator uses a weak-arm cell mean with only one expected treated draw on C; its clipped score on C has O(1) sign noise, so the suboptimal policy 1_{X notin C} is selected with probability bounded away from zero, giving regret bounded below by c P_X(C) tau_0. Hence sup_{P in P_KL} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) is not O(n^{-r_feas}). The honest repair is to restrict to laws where every clipped cell lies inside the hbar_n-margin band, so clipped-cell mistakes cost at most O(hbar_n) per unit mass. That is a genuine smaller law class, not an assumed drift envelope.

changed.from: Full-class lower bound: assuming the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi, M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class (thm:minimax-lower, unconditional). Design-subclass achievability: over the known-logging design subclass P_KL, the feasible ERM attains sup_{P in P_KL} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)} (thm:feasible-upper). Matched within the design: the def:two-point-witness laws can be realized inside P_KL (the construction is a known-logging finite-cell design refined to scale hbar_n), so c n^{-r_star} <= M_n^{KL}(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas} over P_KL. The exponents coincide iff kappa_mu = 1/2: at kappa_mu=1/2 the design-subclass minimax rate is the matched n^{-r_star}; for kappa_mu<1/2 a strict oracle/feasible gap remains. No clean full-class minimax match is claimed.
changed.to: Full-class lower bound: assuming the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi, M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class (thm:minimax-lower, unconditional). Boundary-local design-subclass achievability: over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc from the narrowed thm:feasible-upper, the feasible ERM attains sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}. Matched within that boundary-local design: the def:two-point-witness laws lie in P_KL^loc because on B_n they satisfy p_P=q_n<=2 qbar_n and |tau_P|=h_n<=hbar_n, while off B_n the propensity is 1/2; hence c n^{-r_star} <= M_n^{KL,loc}(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas} over P_KL^loc. The exponents coincide iff kappa_mu = 1/2: at kappa_mu=1/2 the boundary-local design-subclass minimax rate is the matched n^{-r_star}; for kappa_mu<1/2 a strict oracle/feasible gap remains. No clean full-class minimax match is claimed.
changed.reason: The current corollary inherits the false achievability half of thm:feasible-upper. The full-class converse remains valid, but the upper bound is not true on all of P_KL because the frozen primitives allow large-effect clipped cells with only one expected weak-arm sample, creating O(1) sign noise and nonvanishing regret. Narrowing the upper half to the boundary-local subclass P_KL^loc removes exactly that obstruction and still keeps the witness pair inside the design subclass, because s_feas <= 1/(2+alpha+beta_ag) implies h_n <= hbar_n and q_n <= qbar_n for the witness laws.

note: Orchestrator approved round-5 narrowings (full-auto, faithful): thm:feasible-upper + thm:matched-corollary narrowed to the boundary-local design subclass P_KL^loc (rare-arm region carries no large-effect mass: P_X{p<=2 qbar_n, |tau|>hbar_n}=0). This is the intended coupled weak-overlap-near-boundary regime, removes the diameter-0 large-effect single-sample pathology, and keeps the two-point witness inside (h_n<=hbar_n since s_feas<=1/(2+alpha+beta)). Direction-of-truth narrow; converse untouched.

changed.from: Let P_KL^loc be the subclass of laws P in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma) that satisfy ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope, ass:boundary-smoothness, and the boundary-local clipping condition P_X({x : p_P(x) <= 2 qbar_n, |tau_P(x)| > hbar_n}) = 0. Under ass:vc-localized-envelope, the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n of def:feasible-erm satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}, with C>0 independent of n.
changed.to: Let P_KL^{loc,lev} be the subclass of laws P in P_KL^loc such that there exists a constant C_ell>0, independent of n, for which P_X({x : p_P(x) <= 2 qbar_n, max{|mu_0(x)|,|mu_1(x)|} > C_ell hbar_n}) = 0. Under ass:vc-localized-envelope, the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n of def:feasible-erm satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^{loc,lev}} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}, with C>0 independent of n.
changed.reason: As stated, the theorem is false for the frozen estimator. The current class still allows a rare region T_n made of many diameter-zero cells C_j with p_P(C_j)=qbar_n/4, |tau_P(C_j)|=hbar_n, mu_1(C_j)=1, mu_0(C_j)=1-hbar_n, and cell mass m_j=(n p_P(C_j))^{-1}; taking K_n approximately n p_P(C_j) hbar_n^alpha qbar_n^{1/gamma} such cells gives total rare mass M_n approximately hbar_n^alpha qbar_n^{1/gamma}, which saturates ass:overlap-decay and satisfies the boundary-local clipping condition. Add a regular block S_n with p_P=1/2, contrast tau_0>u_0, and mass comparable to M_n, and let Pi contain pi_star=1 and the competing policy that sets treatment 0 on T_n union S_n. On each rare cell, the training-fold weak-arm count is Poisson/Binomial with constant mean, so it is zero with probability bounded away from 0; because def:feasible-erm plugs in the truncated arm-1 empirical mean and the true level mu_1(C_j)=1 while e_P(C_j)<qbar_n, the cross-fitted clipped score on that cell has negative conditional bias of constant order whenever the weak arm is unobserved. Summed over K_n cells, this creates an aggregate empirical-value bonus of order M_n for the suboptimal policy, enough with probability bounded away from zero to outweigh the regular-block welfare loss if the regular-block constant is chosen small. The feasible ERM then selects the suboptimal policy with probability bounded away from zero and suffers regret at least c M_n. But M_n=hbar_n^alpha qbar_n^{1/gamma}=hbar_n^{alpha+beta_ag/gamma}, which dominates hbar_n^{1+alpha}=n^{-r_feas} because beta_ag/gamma<=alpha/(alpha+1)<1 when gamma>0 and equals 0 when gamma=0. Therefore the displayed O(n^{-r_feas}) upper bound is false on P_KL^loc. The proposed narrowing removes exactly this common-level pathology: on clipped cells the arm-specific outcome levels are themselves O(hbar_n), so the empty-weak-arm plug-in bias becomes O(hbar_n) per unit clipped mass rather than O(1).

changed.from: Full-class lower bound: assuming the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi, M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class (thm:minimax-lower, unconditional). Boundary-local design-subclass achievability: over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc from the narrowed thm:feasible-upper, the feasible ERM attains sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}. Matched within that boundary-local design: the def:two-point-witness laws lie in P_KL^loc because on B_n they satisfy p_P=q_n<=2 qbar_n and |tau_P|=h_n<=hbar_n, while off B_n the propensity is 1/2; hence c n^{-r_star} <= M_n^{KL,loc}(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas} over P_KL^loc. The exponents coincide iff kappa_mu = 1/2: at kappa_mu=1/2 the boundary-local design-subclass minimax rate is the matched n^{-r_star}; for kappa_mu<1/2 a strict oracle/feasible gap remains. No clean full-class minimax match is claimed.
changed.to: Full-class lower bound: assuming the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi, M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class (thm:minimax-lower, unconditional). Boundary-local level-controlled design-subclass achievability: over the strengthened subclass P_KL^{loc,lev} from the narrowed thm:feasible-upper, the feasible ERM attains sup_{P in P_KL^{loc,lev}} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,kappa_mu)}. Matched within that strengthened design: the def:two-point-witness laws lie in P_KL^{loc,lev} because on B_n they satisfy p_P=q_n<=2 qbar_n, |tau_P|=h_n<=hbar_n, and max{|mu_0|,|mu_1|}<=h_n, while off B_n the propensity is 1/2; hence c n^{-r_star} <= M_n^{KL,loc,lev}(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas} over P_KL^{loc,lev}. The exponents coincide iff kappa_mu = 1/2: at kappa_mu=1/2 the strengthened boundary-local design-subclass minimax rate is the matched n^{-r_star}; for kappa_mu<1/2 the displayed upper and lower exponents remain separated. No clean full-class minimax match is claimed.
changed.reason: The current corollary inherits the false achievability half of thm:feasible-upper. The full-class minimax lower bound remains valid exactly as stated, but the upper half is not true on all of P_KL^loc because the frozen estimator can be driven by common-level clipped-cell bias coming from empty weak-arm cells, even when |tau_P|<=hbar_n on the entire clipped region. The witness pair itself already satisfies the stronger boundary-local level control (on B_n, mu_0=0 and |mu_1|=h_n), so replacing P_KL^loc by P_KL^{loc,lev} preserves the intended matched-within-design comparison while removing the concrete counterexample that breaks the present upper statement.

note: Approved round-6 narrowing (faithful): thm:feasible-upper/matched-corollary -> P_KL^{loc,lev} (rare-arm region level-controlled, |mu_a|<=C hbar_n; witness satisfies it). Orchestrator ALSO strengthening ass:cell-count-envelope root cause (see next edit): adequate per-cell sampling >= L_n kills the empty-weak-arm-cell O(1)-noise family at the source.

changed.from: Let P_KL^loc be the boundary-local known-logging design subclass: laws in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma) satisfying ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope (granularity + adequate per-cell sampling), and the boundary-local clipping P_X({p_P<=2 qbar_n, |tau_P|>hbar_n})=0. Under ass:vc-localized-envelope, the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n of def:feasible-erm satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, C>0 independent of n.
changed.to: Assume in addition gamma <= alpha + 1. Let P_KL^loc be the boundary-local known-logging design subclass: laws in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma) satisfying ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope (granularity + adequate per-cell sampling), and the boundary-local clipping P_X({p_P<=2 qbar_n, |tau_P|>hbar_n})=0. Under ass:vc-localized-envelope, the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n of def:feasible-erm satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, C>0 independent of n.
changed.reason: As stated the theorem is too strong. There is an explicit shell witness inside the frozen class for every gamma > alpha + 1. Let h=hbar_n, beta=beta_ag=alpha gamma/(alpha+1), and choose a rare block C_n with |tau_P|=h, weak-arm propensity v_n=(c L_n/(n h^alpha))^(gamma/(gamma+1)) <= qbar_n, and mass m_n=c' h^alpha v_n^(1/gamma), so n m_n v_n is of order L_n and the overlap-decay envelope is saturated on that block. Put a regular positive-effect block S_n of mass of order h^E, where E = 1 + alpha + (1 + beta - gamma)/(gamma+1), and let Pi contain the treat-all policy and the competitor that sets treatment 0 on C_n union S_n. On C_n the feasible score uses the weak-arm cell mean with only order-L_n samples, so the training-fold drift contribution m_n Delta_{wk,C_n} fluctuates at scale sqrt(m_n/(n v_n)) = h^E. When gamma > alpha + 1 one has E < 1 + alpha because 1 + beta - gamma = 1 - gamma/(alpha+1) < 0. Choosing the regular block constant small, this random rare-block bonus outweighs the deterministic welfare loss on S_n with probability bounded away from zero, so the ERM selects the suboptimal policy with regret of order h^E = n^{-E/(2+alpha+beta)} >> n^{-(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta)} = n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}. Therefore the displayed O(n^{-r_star}) upper bound fails on the current full gamma-range; the natural honest narrowing is gamma <= alpha + 1, which is exactly the range where this shell obstruction disappears.

changed.from: Assume the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi. Then over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc: the two-point witness (refined to the design granularity) lies in P_KL^loc, so thm:minimax-lower gives M_n^{KL}(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, and thm:feasible-upper gives sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}. Hence the minimax welfare-regret rate is MATCHED, M_n^{KL}(alpha,gamma) asymptotic to n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}). The full-class lower bound M_n(P_class) >= c n^{-r_star} also holds unconditionally.
changed.to: Assume gamma <= alpha + 1 and assume the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi. Then over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc: the two-point witness (refined to the design granularity) lies in P_KL^loc, so thm:minimax-lower gives M_n^{KL}(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, and thm:feasible-upper gives sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}. Hence the minimax welfare-regret rate is MATCHED, M_n^{KL}(alpha,gamma) asymptotic to n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}). The full-class lower bound M_n(P_class) >= c n^{-r_star} also holds unconditionally.
changed.reason: The corollary inherits the achievability defect of thm:feasible-upper. The full-class lower bound from thm:minimax-lower is unaffected, but the upper half and therefore the claimed same-subclass matching fail on the current unrestricted gamma-range because the shell witness in the previous item produces feasible regret strictly larger than n^{-r_star} when gamma > alpha + 1. Adding gamma <= alpha + 1 is therefore a genuine truth-preserving narrowing of the headline matched-rate claim.

note: Approved round-9 narrowing (faithful, full-auto): matched rate restricted to the admissible overlap regime gamma<=alpha+1 (explicit shell counterexample for gamma>alpha+1 gives feasible regret > n^{-r_star} — the boundary-local moderate-overlap obstruction, matching the original run's G_cal). Converse stays unconditional over all gamma; only thm:feasible-upper/matched-corollary gain gamma<=alpha+1. Direction-of-truth narrow.

changed.from: Assume the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi. (i) UNCONDITIONAL CONVERSE: M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class for all gamma (thm:minimax-lower). (ii) ORACLE-MATCHED RATE: for gamma <= alpha+1, over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc, the oracle-nuisance minimax welfare-regret rate is matched, M_n^{oracle,KL}(alpha,gamma) asymptotic to n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) (thm:minimax-lower lower half on the witness in P_KL^loc; thm:oracle-upper upper half). (iii) FEASIBLE GAP (open): the FEASIBLE cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM, which must estimate the weak-arm nuisances from only order n qbar_n effective observations, attains a strictly slower effective-sample rate; whether n^{-r_star} is attainable by ANY feasible estimator under weak overlap is an explicit open problem (the oracle/feasible gap).
changed.to: Assume the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi. (i) UNCONDITIONAL CONVERSE: M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class for all gamma (thm:minimax-lower). (ii) ORACLE UPPER HALF ON THE DESIGN SUBCLASS: for gamma <= alpha+1, over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc, the oracle clipped-AIPW ERM satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n^oracle) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, equivalently M_n^{oracle,KL}(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} (thm:oracle-upper). This shows that the same exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) governs the full-class converse and the oracle design-class achievability, but it does not by itself establish a same-subclass minimax lower bound on P_KL^loc. (iii) FEASIBLE GAP (open): the FEASIBLE cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM, which must estimate the weak-arm nuisances from only order n qbar_n effective observations, attains a strictly slower effective-sample rate; whether n^{-r_star} is attainable by ANY feasible estimator under weak overlap is an explicit open problem (the oracle/feasible gap).
changed.reason: As stated, the corollary overclaims a same-subclass oracle-matched minimax statement. First, on the branch beta_ag=0 one has qbar_n=1/4 and therefore 2 qbar_n=1/2; in def:two-point-witness the off-block region has p_P=1/2 and contrast tau_0>u_0>hbar_n, so the witness law is not in P_KL^loc because it violates the boundary-local clipping condition P_X{p_P<=2 qbar_n, |tau_P|>hbar_n}=0. Second, even when beta_ag>0, the claimed lower half on P_KL^loc needs an explicit refinement of the witness pair into a known partition whose cells all have diameter at most hbar_n and satisfy the rare-cell expected-count condition n m_P(C) p_{a,P}(C) >= C_samp L_n; that design-side realization is not derivable from the present frozen primitives. The proved ingredients therefore yield the unconditional full-class converse plus the oracle design-class upper bound, but not the stronger same-subclass matched minimax conclusion.

note: Approved (faithful, full-auto): thm:matched-corollary narrowed from a same-subclass oracle-matched MINIMAX to the honest rate-characterization — unconditional full-class converse M_n>=c n^{-r_star} (all gamma) + oracle achievability <=C n^{-r_star} on P_KL^loc (gamma<=alpha+1), establishing r_star as the converse rate AND oracle-achievable, WITHOUT a literal same-class equality (the two-point witness is not in P_KL^loc on the beta_ag=0 branch, and the fine-cell witness realization is not derivable from the primitives). Feasible achievability of r_star under weak overlap stays an explicit open problem. Direction-of-truth narrow.

changed.from: Assume the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi. (i) UNCONDITIONAL CONVERSE: M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class for all gamma (thm:minimax-lower). (ii) ORACLE UPPER HALF ON THE DESIGN SUBCLASS: for gamma <= alpha+1, over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc, the oracle clipped-AIPW ERM satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n^oracle) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, equivalently M_n^{oracle,KL}(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} (thm:oracle-upper). This shows that the same exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) governs the full-class converse and the oracle design-class achievability, but it does not by itself establish a same-subclass minimax lower bound on P_KL^loc. (iii) FEASIBLE GAP (open): the FEASIBLE cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM, which must estimate the weak-arm nuisances from only order n qbar_n effective observations, attains a strictly slower effective-sample rate; whether n^{-r_star} is attainable by ANY feasible estimator under weak overlap is an explicit open problem (the oracle/feasible gap).
changed.to: Assume the two witness-optimal policies of def:two-point-witness lie in Pi. (i) UNCONDITIONAL CONVERSE: M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over def:law-class P_class for all gamma (thm:minimax-lower). (ii) ORACLE UPPER HALF ON THE DESIGN SUBCLASS: for gamma <= alpha+1, over the boundary-local known-logging design subclass P_KL^loc, the oracle clipped-AIPW ERM satisfies sup_{P in P_KL^loc} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n^oracle) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, equivalently M_n^{oracle,KL}(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} (thm:oracle-upper). This shows that the same exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) governs the full-class converse and the oracle design-class achievability, but it does not by itself establish a same-subclass minimax lower bound on P_KL^loc. (iii) FEASIBLE GAP (open): whether the FEASIBLE cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM, or any other feasible estimator under weak overlap, can attain n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} is an explicit open problem (the oracle/feasible gap).
changed.reason: Parts (i) and (ii) are immediate from the already-established results thm:minimax-lower and thm:oracle-upper, so the corollary is correct up to the final feasible-gap clause. The trouble is that clause (iii) contains a new positive assertion, namely that the feasible cross-fitted estimator attains a strictly slower rate, but no statement in the frozen dependencies proves any feasible-estimator upper bound or lower bound. The only achievability theorem available in the dependency list is the oracle theorem thm:oracle-upper. Moreover the stale route text still points to a removed feasible-upper theorem, and the surviving definition def:feasible-rate now sets r_feas = r_star rather than introducing a proved slower exponent. So the current clause overstates what the frozen core establishes. The honest narrowing is to retain only the genuinely supported conclusion: the full-class converse and the oracle design-subclass achievability are proved, while the feasible weak-overlap achievability question remains open.

note: Approved (faithful, full-auto): final thm:matched-corollary form — drop the unproven 'feasible is strictly slower' assertion (no dependency proves a feasible bound); state (i) unconditional converse + (ii) oracle achievability on P_KL^loc (same exponent r_star governs both, no same-subclass minimax claim) + (iii) feasible attainability of r_star under weak overlap as a pure open problem. Direction-of-truth narrow; matches the proved deps thm:minimax-lower + thm:oracle-upper exactly.

changed.from: CONSTRUCT-AND-DETERMINE (open solve target). Construct an explicit FEASIBLE estimator of the optimal policy (the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, with a clip/tuning schedule to be CHOSEN by the solver) and DETERMINE the best obtainable worst-case welfare-regret exponent it achieves over the weak-overlap class P_class(alpha,gamma), gamma<=alpha+1: the largest r_feas(alpha,gamma) with sup_{P in P_class} E_P R_P(estimator) <= C n^{-r_feas} (log n)^p. The solver derives r_feas (it is NOT pre-specified): match the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) if attainable; else derive the best LOOSE exponent (e.g. by a bias-variance balance over the clip, using ass:marginal-overlap for the rare-region drift mass and ass:vc-localized-envelope for the oracle offset). Output: the chosen estimator/clip, the derived r_feas, the two-sided bracket c n^{-r_star} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas}, and whether it is tight (at least at the strict-overlap endpoint gamma=0). This is the first welfare-regret rate under coupled Tsybakov margin (alpha) and one-sided overlap decay (gamma), extending strict-overlap policy learning (AtheyWager2021, KitagawaTetenov2018).
changed.to: OPEN: determine the welfare-regret rate attainable by a genuinely feasible estimator, in particular the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, over the weak-overlap class P_class(alpha,gamma), gamma<=alpha+1. The current frozen core proves the full-class converse M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} and the oracle benchmark n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} with true nuisances, but it does not prove a closed-form feasible upper bound. The unresolved issue is whether feasible learning attains the converse exponent n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} or a strictly slower rate because of weak-arm outcome-regression estimation on the clipped region.
changed.reason: As stated, the node overclaims what the frozen primitives support. The feasible proof route does yield a real decomposition: the only new term relative to the oracle theorem is a clipped-region drift driven by weak-arm cell-mean estimation, and that drift can be localized by ass:margin and ass:cell-count-envelope. But closing this into the requested full rate theorem requires a corrected feasible tuning rule and a coherent q-dependent design statement. In the frozen core, qbar_n is wired through def:feasible-rate and ass:cell-count-envelope in a way tailored to the oracle schedule, while the conjecture asks the solver to choose a different feasible clip if needed. Moreover the gamma=0 endpoint is not controlled by ass:marginal-overlap, whose convention for gamma=0 gives no vanishing q-tail to optimize against. So the present core does not justify any final closed-form feasible exponent or two-sided bracket, and the honest mathematical status is an explicit open problem rather than a proved rate theorem.

changed.from: Oracle clipping schedule: hbar_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)}, qbar_n=(1/4 if beta_ag=0 else hbar_n^{beta_ag}), rhobar_n=hbar_n^{1+alpha}. r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_ag) is the converse exponent (thm:minimax-lower) and the oracle-benchmark exponent (thm:oracle-upper). NO feasible-rate exponent is fixed by the primitives (the feasible achievability is open, conj:feasible-rate).
changed.to: Oracle design scalars only: hbar_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)} and qbar_n=(1/4 if beta_ag=0 else hbar_n^{beta_ag}) define the clipping schedule used in the oracle theorem. No feasible exponent r_feas is fixed by the present primitives. Any feasible rate for def:feasible-erm must be derived from an additional balance between the localized empirical-process scale n^{-1/2} q_n^{-1/2} and the clipped-region cell-mean drift, and is therefore left open at the frozen core.
changed.reason: As written, the definition silently recycles the converse/oracle tuning under the symbol r_feas even though the feasible theorem has an extra load-bearing term coming from outcome-regression estimation on the clipped region. That extra term is not encoded in the current construction, so using the displayed hbar_n, qbar_n, and r_feas as if they already described a proved feasible rate makes the core incoherent. The honest correction is to reserve this definition for the oracle clipping schedule and remove any pretense that a feasible exponent has already been fixed.

changed.from: Assume gamma<=alpha+1, ass:marginal-overlap, and ass:feasible-drift-deviation. The FEASIBLE cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n of def:feasible-erm, run with clip schedule qbar_n = n^{-gamma/(1+gamma)}, satisfies sup_{P in P_class(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, where r_feas(alpha,gamma) = (1+alpha)/((1+gamma)(2+alpha)). Equality r_feas=r_star holds at gamma=0 (strict overlap); r_feas<r_star for gamma>0 (a genuine loose upper bound).
changed.to: OPEN: determine the welfare-regret rate attainable by the genuinely feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm under weak overlap. On the stricter known-logging finite-cell design subclass cut out by ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, ass:cell-count-envelope, and ass:marginal-overlap, the present frozen core suggests only the heuristic decomposition of a q-clipped feasible analysis into an oracle-type localized offset term A_n(q) of order n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} polylog(n) and a clipped-region nuisance-learning drift term B_n(q) of order (P_X{p_P<=q}/L_n)^{1/2}. Whether this yields a provable closed-form feasible exponent, and whether any feasible estimator matches the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma), remain open on the present core.
changed.reason: The theorem is not provable as stated for three independent reasons. First, its displayed clip rule qbar_n=n^{-gamma/(1+gamma)} is invalid at gamma=0, where it gives qbar_n=1, outside the admissible clipping range q in (0,1/2] of def:clipped-propensity; thus the claimed strict-overlap endpoint cannot even instantiate the estimator. Second, the supremum is taken over the full law class P_class(alpha,gamma), but def:feasible-erm is an estimator-side known-logging finite-cell construction whose definition relies on the extra design assumptions ass:known-logging-partition, ass:cell-constant-nuisance, and ass:cell-count-envelope, none of which belong to def:law-class. Third, the corrected def:feasible-rate explicitly reserves the feasible rate as open and no longer fixes a closed-form exponent r_feas, so the present theorem's asserted exponent is incoherent with its own frozen dependency. The strongest honest replacement is therefore an open frontier statement recording the feasible-route decomposition rather than a false rate theorem.

changed.from: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:feasible-drift-deviation, Pi containing the witness-optimal policies, and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) (sharp converse, thm:minimax-lower, unconditional all gamma) and r_feas=(1+alpha)/((1+gamma)(2+alpha)) (feasible cross-fit, thm:feasible-upper), r_feas<=r_star with equality iff gamma=0. FIRST welfare-regret rate under weak (one-sided polynomial) overlap, extending strict-overlap policy learning (AtheyWager2021, KitagawaTetenov2018; gamma=0 recovers the strict-overlap exponent). Tight at strict overlap; the gap r_star-r_feas grows with gamma and is the cost of weak-arm nuisance estimation. Tightness for gamma>0 is open (conj:feasible-tight).
changed.to: Under ass:margin-window and assuming Pi contains the two witness-optimal policies used in thm:minimax-lower, the minimax welfare regret satisfies the proved converse bound M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n, where r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}). Determining any matching or loose feasible upper bound for the cross-fitted estimator of def:feasible-erm under weak overlap is open on the present frozen core; in particular no closed-form feasible exponent r_feas is proved here.
changed.reason: The current two-sided bracket depends on thm:feasible-upper, but that upper-rate theorem is false/incoherent on the frozen core. Once the feasible theorem is removed, the asserted exponent r_feas and the qualitative claim 'tight at strict overlap, gap for gamma>0' lose their evidentiary support. The surviving proved content is exactly the converse half supplied by thm:minimax-lower. The honest narrowing is therefore to keep the lower-bound characterization and state the feasible upper problem as open rather than claiming a bracket that the dependencies do not establish.

changed.from: OPEN (close the gap for gamma>0): can the feasible exponent be improved from r_feas=(1+alpha)/((1+gamma)(2+alpha)) to the converse r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) (a better estimator), or is the converse loose? The gap is zero at gamma=0 and grows with gamma.
changed.to: OPEN: determine the welfare-regret rate attainable by a genuinely feasible estimator, in particular the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, under weak overlap. Can any feasible estimator attain the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma), or must every feasible procedure be strictly slower because the weak-arm outcome regression must be learned on the clipped region?
changed.reason: The current conjecture presupposes a proved benchmark exponent r_feas and a proved gap r_star-r_feas. After the feasible upper theorem is withdrawn, neither the displayed feasible exponent nor the claimed shape of the gap is established by the frozen core. The honest residual open problem is therefore the attainability of the converse exponent itself, not the improvement of a presently unproved formula.

note: TERMINAL (orchestrator, full-auto): feasible upper bound is a genuine in-pipeline wall (~8 rejections across matched/oracle/loose/gated framings; legitimate reasons: clip invalid at gamma=0, feasible ERM lives on the known-logging subclass not the full class, closed-form r_feas not derivable). Accept the solver's honest open-frontier reframe: keep the SHARP CONVERSE (thm:minimax-lower, first weak-overlap policy-regret lower bound) + feasible rate as an EXPLICIT OPEN problem with the route decomposition recorded. No feasible-rate over-claim.

changed.from: Exact clipped-score drift identity. For the cross-fit plug-in nuisances etabar=(mubar_0,mubar_1,ebar) with ebar_q=min{1-q,max{q,ebar}} and Delta_a=mubar_a-mu_a, the conditional-mean drift of def:clipped-aipw-score is exactly b_q(x):=E_P[Gamma_q(O;etabar)|X=x]-tau_P(x) = (ebar_q(x)-e_P(x))(Delta_1(x)/ebar_q(x) + Delta_0(x)/(1-ebar_q(x))). It cancels only where ebar_q=e_P (or Delta_0=Delta_1=0); it does NOT vanish merely on {p_P>q}. Consequently the policy-weighted drift is the product bound |E_P[(pi-pi_star) b_q]| <= ||ebar_q-e_P||_{L2} * ||Delta/ebar_q||_{L2} (Cauchy-Schwarz), with ebar_q >= q.
changed.to:
Exact clipped-score drift identity. For any measurable plug-in nuisance triple \(\bar\eta=(\bar\mu_0,\bar\mu_1,\bar e)\), with \(\bar e_q=\min\{1-q,\max\{q,\bar e\}\}\) and \(\Delta_a=\bar\mu_a-\mu_a\), the conditional mean drift satisfies
\[
E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\bar\eta)\mid X=x]-\tau_P(x)
=(\bar e_q(x)-e_P(x))\left(\frac{\Delta_1(x)}{\bar e_q(x)}+\frac{\Delta_0(x)}{1-\bar e_q(x)}\right).
\]
Hence cancellation holds only where \(\bar e_q(x)=e_P(x)\) or both regression errors vanish; it does not follow merely from \(p_P(x)>q\). Under the frozen assumptions alone, no additive clipped-region bound of the form asserted in the current lemma is derivable.
changed.reason: The current lemma is algebraically false for the estimator defined by def:feasible-erm. With estimated nuisances, the clipped AIPW conditional mean differs from \(\tau_P\) by a product term involving both propensity error and regression error. The proposed narrowing replaces the false off-clipped-region cancellation claim by the exact identity derivable from def:clipped-aipw-score.
